name: BTC 4h State Change Hold Expansion v1
edge_mechanism: directional improvement appears after a reset, fast structure is reclaimed, the reclaim holds on the next bar, and expansion resumes from that improved state
thesis: The last five cycles still point to transition-friendly control-change structures as one of the few promising directions, but lane fit has to be real. BTC 4h is the better owner here than ETH because it offers more transition character while staying inside the allowed basket. The grammar is deliberately simple: reclaim, one-bar hold, then expansion.
invalidation_condition: If BTC 4h reclaim-hold sequences remain too sparse or repeatedly fail before modest expansion, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price recovers from a reset, reclaims fast structure, holds it for one more bar, and expands as directional control improves.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition-capable markets often show a clean handoff from chop back into directional movement. A one-bar hold after reclaim should capture that handoff without turning the idea into a ceremonial sparse chain.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is the best fit because it provides more transition structure than ETH 4h while remaining a cleaner, more liquid lane than AXS or TAO for this specific state-change test.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: Late reclaim entries can become local exhaustion buys if the impulse is already spent.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If trades exist but runner contribution is poor, move more size to the first partial before changing the reclaim grammar.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: A reasonable number of reclaim-hold entries, several first-target wins, and a smaller number of stronger expansion runners.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: BTC 4h clears moderate density and produces materially better participation than recent sparse 4h control-change families.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-15 trades, or repeated hold confirmations that fail before first target.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because the hold requirement is only one next-bar check

validation_basket.why_included: Best-fit mixed chop/transition lane for reclaim-hold state change.
validation_basket.continue_if: BTC 4h shows enough reclaim-hold expansions with positive first-target expectancy.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h entries remain too sparse or fail immediately.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Moderate count with several continuation bursts.

validation_basket.why_included: Useful control to see whether pure chop suppresses or preserves the same reclaim-hold idea.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h still prints a tradable but smaller version of the state-change grammar.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h confirms the idea needs more transition than pure chop offers.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude, slightly lower count continuation than BTC.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether stronger trend contamination improves or degrades the same state-change hold behavior.
validation_basket.continue_if: TAO 4h keeps enough reclaim-hold continuation without turning into late chase entries.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h converts the setup into exhausted continuation buying.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower count, stronger carry than BTC.

long: Next bar low stays >= EMA_20 - 0.07 * ATR_14 and close remains >= EMA_20
short: Next bar high stays <= EMA_20 + 0.07 * ATR_14 and close remains <= EMA_20
